<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

I have a good picture. Let me read the rest of resizeEvent (optionsButton and the elided text part) and confirm the flow. I also need to decide:

1. **Custom button storage**: Store custom buttons in a list or dict on DockLabel, e.g. `self.custom_buttons: Dict[str, QToolButton]` keyed by the config key so we can update/remove when config changes.

2. **When to create/update custom buttons**: 
   - Option A: Create all custom buttons in DockLabel.__init__ from config.custom_button_configs (like we do for fixed buttons), and show/hide in updateButtonsFromConfig. If config.custom_button_configs changes later, we need to add/remove buttons — so we need a method to rebuild custom buttons from config.
   - Option B: Build custom buttons only in updateButtonsFromConfig (create/destroy QToolButtons when config changes). This keeps one source of truth but requires creating/destroying widgets when config changes.

   Better approach: In __init__, create no custom buttons. Add a method _buildCustomButtons() that creates QToolButton for each entry in config.custom_button_configs (where showButton is True), stores them in self.custom_buttons dict, connects clicked to a lambda that emits sigCustomButtonClicked(button_id). Call _buildCustomButtons from updateButtonsFromConfig, and in updateButtonsFromConfig first remove/delete any existing custom buttons then rebuild. That way when config changes we always reflect it.

   Actually: we could create custom buttons once in __init__ by iterating config.custom_button_configs, and in updateButtonsFromConfig we only set visibility (showButton) and add/remove buttons if the dict changed. That's more efficient. So: 
   - __init__: for each key, cfg in config.custom_button_configs.items(): create QToolButton, set icon/tooltip from cfg, connect to a slot that will emit with key. Store in self.custom_buttons: Dict[str, QToolButton].
   - updateButtonsFromConfig: set visibility for each custom button from cfg.showButton; also add new buttons for any new keys in config, remove widgets for keys that were removed. So we need to support dynamic add/remove. Easier: in updateButtonsFromConfig, clear existing custom buttons (hide and disconnect, or delete) and rebuild from config.custom_button_configs. That way we don't need to track which were added/removed. Rebuilding is simple and dict order is preserved (Python 3.7+).

3. **Order of buttons**: Standard buttons have a fixed order (close, collapse, orientation, group, timelineSyncMode, options). Custom buttons should appear after options (or we could add a "position" in DockButtonConfig later). For now: custom buttons after options, in iteration order of custom_button_configs.

4. **Layout in resizeEvent**: After positioning optionsButton, iterate self.custom_buttons (use consistent order — e.g. sorted keys or list of keys in display order). For each visible custom button, position it with current_x/current_y and advance.

5. **button_occupied_space**: num_total_title_bar_buttons must include the count of visible custom buttons. So in updateButtonsFromConfig we set num_total_title_bar_buttons = (fixed count) + sum(1 for cfg in self.config.custom_button_configs.values() if cfg.showButton).

6. **Dock side**: Add sigCustomButtonClicked = QtCore.Signal(object, str)  # (dock, button_id). In Dock.__init__, connect self.label.sigCustomButtonClicked to a slot that either calls the DockButtonConfig.buttonCallback if provided or just emits sigCustomButtonClicked. So the callback can be on the config (called from Dock when button is clicked) or listeners can connect to Dock.sigCustomButtonClicked.

   DockButtonConfig has buttonCallback: Callable. So when a custom button is clicked, we can either (1) call config's buttonCallback(dock, button_id) from Dock, or (2) emit a signal and let the owner connect. Both is best: if buttonCallback is set, call it; and always emit sigCustomButtonClicked so others can react.

7. **Shortcut**: DockButtonConfig has buttonShortcut and buttonShortcutContext. We need to create QShortcut on the Dock or DockLabel and connect to the same handler. So when the shortcut is triggered, we emit the same as button click. Do this in Dock (so shortcut is in context of the dock).

8. **Context menu**: Should we add "Show dock buttons" entries for each custom button? That would require updateButtonVisibility to support button_type like 'custom_<key>'. Optional for later; not in initial plan unless user wants it.

Let me also check: DockButtonConfig uses QtWidgets.QStyle.StandardPixmap and QtCore.Qt.ShortcutContext — these are PyQt/PySide types. The _embed/dock_display_config.py doesn't have DockButtonConfig or custom_button_configs; the full DockDisplayConfig in Dock.py does. So all changes stay in Dock.py.

Creating the plan.
</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

I have everything. One more thing: the condition that sets button_size is "if self.closeButton or self.collapseButton or ...". We need to include "or self.custom_buttons" (or "or getattr(self, 'custom_buttons', {})") so that when only custom buttons are visible we still get a button_size. Actually when only custom buttons exist, we need button_size to be computed. So the condition should be extended to "or (getattr(self, 'custom_buttons', None) and any(b.isVisible() for b in self.custom_buttons.values()))" or simpler: just "or getattr(self, 'custom_buttons', {})" — if there are custom buttons we need size. So: `if self.closeButton or ... or self.optionsButton or (getattr(self, 'custom_buttons', {}))`.

Let me also consider: should custom buttons be created in __init__ or lazily in updateButtonsFromConfig? If we create in __init__, we iterate config.custom_button_configs and create buttons. Then in updateButtonsFromConfig we set their visibility and update icon/tooltip from config (in case config was mutated). If config.custom_button_configs gets new keys later, we need to create new buttons — so we need a method that ensures self.custom_buttons has a button for each key in config, and removes buttons for keys no longer in config. That's updateButtonsFromConfig's job: (1) sync self.custom_buttons with config.custom_button_configs (create new, delete removed), (2) set visibility and props from each cfg, (3) update num_total_title_bar_buttons. So we need _ensureCustomButtons() or do it inline in updateButtonsFromConfig.

Implementation outline:

**DockLabel**
- __init__: self.custom_buttons: Dict[str, QToolButton] = {}. Call _buildCustomButtons() at end (after updateButtonsFromConfig) so custom buttons are created after fixed buttons.
- Add sigCustomButtonClicked = QtCore.Signal(object, str)  # (dock, button_id) — actually the label doesn't have dock reference... it has self.dock. So emit sigCustomButtonClicked(button_id) and Dock can connect and emit (self, button_id). So DockLabel.sigCustomButtonClicked(str) and Dock connects and emits sigCustomButtonClicked(self, str).
- _buildCustomButtons(): For each key, cfg in config.custom_button_configs.items(): create QToolButton, set icon from cfg.buttonIcon, tooltip from cfg.buttonToolTip, visibility from cfg.showButton; connect clicked to lambda k=key: self._on_custom_button_clicked(k). Store in self.custom_buttons[key]. Before that, remove any buttons whose keys are no longer in config (deleteLater). So: for key in list(self.custom_buttons): if key not in config.custom_button_configs: self.custom_buttons[key].deleteLater(); del self.custom_buttons[key]. For key in config.custom_button_configs: if key not in self.custom_buttons: create button and add. Then for each key set visibility and icon/tooltip from config.
- updateButtonsFromConfig: after setting fixed button visibility and num_total_title_bar_buttons, call _buildCustomButtons() (or inline the sync logic), then add to num_total_title_bar_buttons the count of visible custom buttons. So: num_total_title_bar_buttons = fixed_count + sum(1 for c in self.config.custom_button_configs.values() if c.showButton).
- resizeEvent: after optionsButton block, for key in (sorted(self.custom_buttons.keys()) or iteration order): btn = self.custom_buttons[key]; if not btn.isVisible(): continue; position btn with current_x/current_y; advance. Also extend the "if self.closeButton or ..." condition to set button_size when we have custom buttons.
- _on_custom_button_clicked(self, button_id: str): self.sigCustomButtonClicked.emit(button_id)

**Dock**
- sigCustomButtonClicked = QtCore.Signal(object, str)  # (dock, button_id)
- __init__: self.label.sigCustomButtonClicked.connect(self._on_custom_button_clicked)
- _on_custom_button_clicked(self, button_id: str): cfg = self.config.custom_button_configs.get(button_id); if cfg and cfg.buttonCallback: cfg.buttonCallback(self) or cfg.buttonCallback(self, button_id)? The signature could be (dock) or (dock, button_id). I'll pass (self, button_id). Then self.sigCustomButtonClicked.emit(self, button_id).
- Shortcuts: In Dock.__init__, for each key, cfg in config.custom_button_configs: if cfg.buttonShortcut: QShortcut(cfg.buttonShortcut, self, context=cfg.buttonShortcutContext, activated=lambda k=key: self._on_custom_button_clicked(k)). Store shortcuts so we can update if config changes? For simplicity, create shortcuts in __init__ and when config changes we'd need to update — or create shortcuts in a method that's called from __init__ and when custom_button_configs changes. For minimal first version, create shortcuts in Dock.__init__ for each custom button that has a shortcut. Store in self._custom_button_shortcuts: Dict[str, QShortcut] and update when config changes (optional for later).

**Context menu**: Optional — add "Show dock buttons" submenu entries for custom buttons (toggle visibility). That would require DockDisplayConfig to support per-button show state (we have DockButtonConfig.showButton). So updateButtonVisibility could accept 'custom_<key>' and set config.custom_button_configs[key].showButton = visible, then label.updateButtonsFromConfig(). I'll include this in the plan as optional/follow-up.

**Order of custom buttons**: Use dict iteration order (Python 3.7+ preserves insertion order). So in resizeEvent iterate self.custom_buttons in the order we want. We need a stable order: we could store the list of keys when we build so that order = list(config.custom_button_configs.keys()). So in resizeEvent use that order. Actually self.custom_buttons is a dict; iteration order is insertion order. When we _buildCustomButtons we add in config.custom_button_configs.items() order, and when we remove we delete from dict. So iteration over self.custom_buttons is the order we added. Good.

I'll now write the plan.
</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

So `dynamic_dock_display_area.py` and the package `__init__` use `Dock` and `DockDisplayConfig` from `EXTERNAL.pyqtgraph.dockarea.Dock`. The `dock_display_configs.py` uses `_embed.dock_display_config.DockDisplayConfig` (the minimal one). So there are two DockDisplayConfig classes: the minimal one in _embed (no custom_button_configs) and the full one in EXTERNAL/pyqtgraph/dockarea/Dock.py (with custom_button_configs). Code that uses the EXTERNAL Dock gets the full DockDisplayConfig from that file. So we only need to implement in [Dock.py](pypho_timeline/EXTERNAL/pyqtgraph/dockarea/Dock.py). The _embed version doesn't have custom_button_configs and doesn't need to for minimal use. Plan is complete.
</details></think>

Checking how `dock_display_configs.CustomDockDisplayConfig` relates to the EXTERNAL `DockDisplayConfig`:


---

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="read_file_v2">
<details><summary>Tool use: **read_file_v2** • Read file: C:\Users\pho\repos\EmotivEpoc\ACTIVE_DEV\pyPhoTimeline\pypho_timeline\docking\dock_display_configs.py</summary>

</tool-use>

---

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

CustomDockDisplayConfig subclasses the _embed DockDisplayConfig. When a Dock is created with a config from dock_display_configs, that config might be the _embed one (no custom_button_configs). So the EXTERNAL Dock.py defines its own full DockDisplayConfig with custom_button_configs. When the app uses dynamic_dock_display_area it imports Dock, DockDisplayConfig from EXTERNAL - so it gets the full one. When the app uses CustomDockDisplayConfig from dock_display_configs, that subclasses _embed.DockDisplayConfig which doesn't have custom_button_configs. So if someone passes CustomDockDisplayConfig to Dock(), that config might not have custom_button_configs attribute. We need to make the Dock/DockLabel code defensive: use getattr(config, 'custom_button_configs', {}) so that configs that don't have it (e.g. _embed or subclasses) still work. I'll add that to the plan.
</details></think>

<details>
<summary>Tool use: **create_plan**</summary>


Parameters:

```json
{
  "plan": "# Custom dock buttons from DockDisplayConfig\n\n## Scope\n\n- **Single file**: [pypho_timeline/EXTERNAL/pyqtgraph/dockarea/Dock.py](pypho_timeline/EXTERNAL/pyqtgraph/dockarea/Dock.py). The full &#96;DockDisplayConfig&#96; and &#96;DockButtonConfig&#96; are defined here; &#96;_embed/dock_display_config.py&#96; has no &#96;custom_button_configs&#96; and is not modified.\n- **Config compatibility**: Use &#96;getattr(self.config, 'custom_button_configs', {})&#96; so configs that lack this attribute (e.g. from &#96;_embed&#96; or &#96;dock_display_configs.CustomDockDisplayConfig&#96;) still work.\n\n## Data flow\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;mermaid\nflowchart LR\n  subgraph config [DockDisplayConfig]\n    CBC[custom_button_configs]\n  end\n  subgraph label [DockLabel]\n    CB[custom_buttons dict]\n    SIG[sigCustomButtonClicked]\n  end\n  subgraph dock [Dock]\n    SCB[sigCustomButtonClicked]\n    CBK[buttonCallback]\n  end\n  CBC --&gt; label\n  label --&gt; CB\n  CB --&gt; SIG\n  SIG --&gt; dock\n  dock --&gt; CBK\n  dock --&gt; SCB\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## 1. DockLabel: custom button storage and creation\n\n- **Attribute**: Add &#96;self.custom_buttons: Dict[str, QtWidgets.QToolButton] = {}&#96; in &#96;DockLabel.__init__&#96; (after existing button setup).\n- **Signal**: Add &#96;sigCustomButtonClicked = QtCore.Signal(str)&#96; (emits &#96;button_id&#96;).\n- **New method &#96;_buildCustomButtons()&#96;** (call from &#96;updateButtonsFromConfig&#96;):\n  - Read &#96;custom_button_configs = getattr(self.config, 'custom_button_configs', {})&#96;.\n  - Remove buttons whose key is no longer in config: for each key in a copy of &#96;self.custom_buttons&#96;, if key not in config, call &#96;btn.deleteLater()&#96;, then &#96;del self.custom_buttons[key]&#96;.\n  - For each key in &#96;custom_button_configs&#96;: if key not in &#96;self.custom_buttons&#96;, create a &#96;QToolButton&#96;, set icon via &#96;QtWidgets.QApplication.style().standardIcon(cfg.buttonIcon)&#96;, set tooltip from &#96;cfg.buttonToolTip&#96;, set min/fixed size to &#96;MIN_BUTTON_SIZE&#96; (12), connect &#96;clicked&#96; to a lambda that calls &#96;self._on_custom_button_clicked(key)&#96;, set parent to &#96;self&#96;, add to &#96;self.custom_buttons[key]&#96;.\n  - For each key, update visibility from &#96;cfg.showButton&#96;, and optionally refresh icon/tooltip from config.\n- **&#96;_on_custom_button_clicked(self, button_id: str)&#96;**: Emit &#96;self.sigCustomButtonClicked.emit(button_id)&#96;.\n\n## 2. DockLabel: layout and count\n\n- **&#96;updateButtonsFromConfig()&#96;**: After setting visibility of fixed buttons and computing the fixed button count, call &#96;_buildCustomButtons()&#96;. Then set:\n  - &#96;num_visible_custom = sum(1 for c in getattr(self.config, 'custom_button_configs', {}).values() if c.showButton)&#96;\n  - &#96;self.num_total_title_bar_buttons = (fixed_count + num_visible_custom)&#96;.\n- **&#96;resizeEvent()&#96;**:\n  - In the condition that sets &#96;button_size&#96; (line ~1221), extend so &#96;button_size&#96; is also set when &#96;getattr(self, 'custom_buttons', {})&#96; is non-empty (so layout works when only custom buttons are visible).\n  - After the &#96;# END if self.optionsButton...&#96; block, add a loop over &#96;self.custom_buttons&#96; (iteration order = insertion order): for each button, if not &#96;btn.isVisible()&#96; skip; else &#96;setFixedSize(button_size, button_size)&#96;, then &#96;move(current_x, 0)&#96; or &#96;move(0, current_y)&#96; depending on orientation, and advance &#96;current_x&#96; or &#96;current_y&#96; by &#96;button_size&#96;.\n\n## 3. Dock: signal and callback\n\n- **Signal**: Add &#96;sigCustomButtonClicked = QtCore.Signal(object, str)&#96; (dock, button_id) on &#96;Dock&#96;.\n- **In &#96;Dock.__init__&#96;**: Connect &#96;self.label.sigCustomButtonClicked.connect(self._on_custom_button_clicked)&#96;.\n- **&#96;_on_custom_button_clicked(self, button_id: str)&#96;**:\n  - Emit &#96;self.sigCustomButtonClicked.emit(self, button_id)&#96;.\n  - Get &#96;cfg = getattr(self.config, 'custom_button_configs', {}).get(button_id)&#96;; if &#96;cfg&#96; and &#96;cfg.buttonCallback&#96; is callable, call &#96;cfg.buttonCallback(self, button_id)&#96; (or &#96;cfg.buttonCallback(self)&#96; if callback arity is 1; support both for compatibility).\n\n## 4. Optional: keyboard shortcuts\n\n- **Dock**: In &#96;__init__&#96;, after the label is set up, for each &#96;(key, cfg)&#96; in &#96;getattr(display_config, 'custom_button_configs', {}).items()&#96;: if &#96;cfg.buttonShortcut&#96; is non-empty, create &#96;QtWidgets.QShortcut(QtGui.QKeySequence(cfg.buttonShortcut), self, cfg.buttonShortcutContext, activated=lambda k=key: self._on_custom_button_clicked(k))&#96;. Store in &#96;self._custom_button_shortcuts: Dict[str, QShortcut]&#96; so they can be updated if config changes later (optional). If not implementing update-on-config-change, creating shortcuts once in &#96;__init__&#96; is enough.\n\n## 5. Context menu (optional / follow-up)\n\n- **&#96;buildContextMenu&#96;**: Under \"Show dock buttons\", add checkable entries for each custom button (e.g. \"Custom: &amp;lt;key&amp;gt;\") that toggle &#96;config.custom_button_configs[key].showButton&#96; and call &#96;self.label.updateButtonsFromConfig()&#96;.\n- **&#96;updateButtonVisibility&#96;**: Extend to support &#96;button_type == 'custom_&lt;key&gt;'&#96; by setting &#96;self.config.custom_button_configs[key].showButton = visible&#96; and calling &#96;self.label.updateButtonsFromConfig()&#96; (only if we add context menu toggles).\n\n## 6. Debug and exports\n\n- **&#96;debug_dock_label&#96;**: Optionally log presence and count of &#96;custom_buttons&#96; for diagnostics.\n- **Exports**: Ensure &#96;DockButtonConfig&#96; (and any new signals) are part of the public API if other modules need to type-hint or connect (no change to &#96;__init__.py&#96; required if already importing from this file).\n\n## Implementation order\n\n1. DockLabel: &#96;custom_buttons&#96; dict, &#96;sigCustomButtonClicked&#96;, &#96;_buildCustomButtons()&#96;, &#96;_on_custom_button_clicked()&#96;.\n2. DockLabel: Call &#96;_buildCustomButtons()&#96; from &#96;updateButtonsFromConfig()&#96; and include custom count in &#96;num_total_title_bar_buttons&#96;.\n3. DockLabel: In &#96;resizeEvent()&#96;, extend &#96;button_size&#96; condition and add custom-button positioning loop.\n4. Dock: Add &#96;sigCustomButtonClicked&#96;, connect in &#96;__init__&#96;, implement &#96;_on_custom_button_clicked()&#96; (emit + optional callback).\n5. Optional: shortcuts in Dock &#96;__init__&#96;; optional: context menu and &#96;updateButtonVisibility&#96; for custom keys.\n\n## Key code locations\n\n| What | Where |\n|------|--------|\n| &#96;DockButtonConfig&#96; | [Dock.py](pypho_timeline/EXTERNAL/pyqtgraph/dockarea/Dock.py) ~78–85 |\n| &#96;DockDisplayConfig.custom_button_configs&#96; | [Dock.py](pypho_timeline/EXTERNAL/pyqtgraph/dockarea/Dock.py) ~103 |\n| DockLabel button creation | [Dock.py](pypho_timeline/EXTERNAL/pyqtgraph/dockarea/Dock.py) ~1044–1100 |\n| DockLabel.updateButtonsFromConfig | [Dock.py](pypho_timeline/EXTERNAL/pyqtgraph/dockarea/Dock.py) ~1126–1157 |\n| DockLabel.resizeEvent | [Dock.py](pypho_timeline/EXTERNAL/pyqtgraph/dockarea/Dock.py) ~1212–1362 |\n| Dock signal connections in __init__ | [Dock.py](pypho_timeline/EXTERNAL/pyqtgraph/dockarea/Dock.py) ~258–272 |\n",
  "name": "Custom dock buttons support",
  "overview": "Add support for rendering and handling custom title-bar buttons defined in &#96;DockDisplayConfig.custom_button_configs&#96; (dict of &#96;DockButtonConfig&#96;), with dynamic layout in DockLabel and signal/callback handling in Dock."
}
```
